titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5980

p.seg: To all who shall view or hear this written document, Mariota of Chirnside, formerly the wife of Richard of Renton, and Patrick her son send greeting.

Know you all that since we have taken the prior and convent of Coldingham to court, before the lord Walter Oliphant, then justiciar of Lothian, by a brieve of recognition of the lord king, concerning one ploughgate of land with pertinents in Renton, we have quitclaimed and renounced in full court, in respect of ourselves and our heirs, all our right and claim, if we had or could have had any, to the said prior and convent and their successors.
As witness of this matter, we have put our seals to this present document.

With these witnesses: the lord W. of Lindsay, Ranulf of Bunkle, the lord Ralph Noble, the lord William of Mordington, the lord Alan of Swinton, Alan his son, Robert and William of Paxton, Adam of Prenderguest, Adam son of John of Ayton, Bertram of Reston, John son of Ingram, and others.
